notes:
NI Scarbee bass series — four deeply sampled electric basses in Kontakt, each available in DI and Amped variants. All share the same CC/QC layout. Per-instrument character:

  MM-Bass (Music Man StingRay): Single humbucker, active EQ, punchy and scooped. The most aggressive Scarbee bass. Amped variant adds a cranked amp simulation. QC1 = Pickup Balance, QC5 = Slap Layer Blend (unique to MM-Bass).

  Pre-Bass (Fender Precision): Split-coil pickup, classic thumpy midrange. The standard studio rock/pop bass. Smooth finger tone. Amped adds warm amp grit.

  Jay-Bass (Fender Jazz): Two single-coil pickups, QC1 blends between neck (warm/round) and bridge (bright/cutting). Extremely versatile — the most balanced of the four.

  Rickenbacker Bass (4001): Distinctive treble grind and piano-like upper harmonics. Two pickups: neck (warm) and bridge (harsh, nasal). The Chris Squire/Paul McCartney bass. Amped variant critical for getting the full Rickenbacker character.

All variants respond to velocity for pick/pluck hardness. Legato passages require overlapping note input for slides. Right-click any QC → Learn MIDI CC to confirm assignments in your loaded patch.

Expression.description: Expression — primary dynamics/output level. Use for verse/chorus level shifts and natural breath between phrases.

Modulation.description: Performance variation — finger vs pick style blend or playing intensity (patch-dependent)

Sustain.description: Sustain pedal — holds note / enables legato slide mode on some patches

Quick Control 1.description: Pickup Balance / Position — neck (warm, round) vs bridge (bright, cutting). MM-Bass: humbucker blend. Jay-Bass: neck vs bridge single-coil blend. Rickenbacker: neck vs bridge character.

Quick Control 2.description: Tone — overall tonal brightness / passive tone control simulation. Low = dark and vintage. High = modern hi-fi snap with more upper harmonic detail.

Quick Control 3.description: Amp Character / DI Blend — Amped variants: clean DI vs amp cabinet drive blend. DI variants: output EQ character. High = more amp warmth and harmonic grit.

Quick Control 4.description: Reverb / Room Amount — built-in ambience. Keep subtle for tight mix bass; raise for vintage or dub-style depth.

Quick Control 5.description: Attack / Playing Style — pick vs finger attack hardness, or on MM-Bass Amped: slap layer blend. Controls the sharpness of the initial transient.

Quick Control 6.description: Release / Sustain Time — note decay tail. Short = staccato, tight modern bass. Long = sustaining, singing legato tone.

Quick Control 7.description: Fret / String Noise — mechanical bass noise: fret buzz, finger slides, string squeak. Essential for realism; keep low in dense mixes, higher for exposed bass lines.

Quick Control 8.description: Humanise — random velocity, timing, and intonation variation between notes for a more played-in feel

Portamento.description: Portamento/glide time for legato bass slides
